    public int bitwiseComplement(int n) {

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int bitwiseComplement(int n) {
        if (n == 0) return 1;
        int bitLength = Integer.bitLength(n);
        int mask = (1 << bitLength) - 1;
        return n ^ mask;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution calculates the complement by determining the bit-length of the input and XORing it with a mask of all 1s of the same length. This efficiently flips only the relevant bits. Handles n=0 as a special case.
